mission.title: Install and Configure Security Compliance on Kubernetes
mission.description: Zot is a production-ready vendor-neutral OCI image registry that allows you to store and manage container images in a compliant manner. Installing Zot will enable you to have a reliable and scalable solution for your container image storage needs.

steps.title: Add the Zot Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Zot Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add zot https://zotregistry.dev/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Zot using Helm
steps.description:
Install Zot in a new namespace called 'zot' with the specified version:
```bash
helm install zot zot/zot --version v2.1.14 --namespace zot --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Zot pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace zot
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the Zot deployment to set resource limits:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment zot --namespace zot
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access the Zot service
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the Zot service locally:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/zot --namespace zot 8080:8080
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Zot Helm release from the cluster:
```bash
helm uninstall zot --namespace zot
```

uninstall.title: Delete the Zot namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the Zot namespace to clean up all associated resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace zot
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's a good practice to backup the current configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace zot -o yaml > zot-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Zot to the latest version
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Zot installation to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade zot zot/zot --version v2.1.14 --namespace zot
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Zot pods are running after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace zot
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace zot
# Look for events indicating insufficient resources or scheduling issues.
```

troubleshooting.title: Zot service not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Zot service, verify the port-forwarding command:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace zot
# Ensure the service is running and the port is correct.
```

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff errors
troubleshooting.description:
If you see CrashLoopBackOff errors, check the pod logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace zot
# Review the logs for any application errors that may indicate misconfiguration.
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Zot will have all pods in the 'zot' namespace running and accessible via the specified port. You should also be able to configure resource limits and access the service through port-forwarding.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add zot https://zotregistry.dev/charts
helm repo update
helm install zot zot/zot --version v2.1.14 --namespace zot --create-namespace

kubectl edit deployment zot --namespace zot
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed.
